name: nextflow
description: Nextflow enables scalable and reproducible scientific workflows using software containers. It allows the adaptation of pipelines written in the most common scripting languages

subcommands.name: clean
subcommands.description: Clean up project cache and work directories (Default: last run)

options.description: Clean up runs executed after the specified one
Clean up runs executed before the specified one
Clean up all runs except the specified one
Print names of file to be removed without deleting them (Default: false)
Force clean command (Default: false)
Print the command usage (Default: false)
Removes only temporary files but retains execution log entries and metadata (Default: false)
Do not print names of files removed (Default: false)

subcommands.name: clone
subcommands.description: Clone a project into a folder

options.description: Print the command usage (Default: false)
Service hub where the project is hosted (Default: GitHub)
Revision to clone - It can be a git branch, tag or revision number
Private repository user name
Print config using flat notation (Default: false)
Print the command usage (Default: false)
Choose a configuration profile
Prints config using Java properties notatio (Default: false)
Show all configuration profiles
Sort config attributes (Default: false)
Print the command usage (Default: false)

subcommands.name: drop
subcommands.description: Delete the local copy of a project

options.description: Print the command usage (Default: false)
Delete the repository without taking care of local changes (Default: false)

args.name: name of the project to drop

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Print the usage help for a command

options.description: Print the command usage (Default: false)

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Print project and system runtime information

options.description: Check for remote updates (Default: false)
Show detailed information (Default: false)
Print the command usage (Default: false)
Output format, either: text (default), json, yaml

subcommands.name: kuberun
subcommands.description: Execute a workflow in a Kubernetes cluster (experimental)

options.description: Exports all current system environment (Default: false)
Enable/disable ANSI console logging
Remote bucket where intermediate result files are stored
Enable/disable processes caching
Prevent the cancellation of child jobs on execution termination
Execute the workflow using DSL1 syntax (Default: false)
Execute the workflow using DSL2 syntax (Default: false)
Dump channels for debugging purpose
Dump task hash keys for debugging purpose (Default: false)
Add the specified variable to execution environment. Syntax: -e.key=value (Default: {})
Entry workflow name to be executed
Specify number of CPUs requested for the Nextflow pod (Default: 0)
Specify amount of memory requested for the Nextflow pod
Print the command usage (Default: false)
Service hub where the project is hosted (Default: github)
Pull latest changes before run (Default: false)
Library extension path
The script file to be executed when launching a project directory or repository
Assign a mnemonic name to the a pipeline run
Specify the K8s namespace to use
Do not check for remote project updates (Default: false)
Load script parameters from a JSON/YAML file
Specify the plugins to be applied for this run e.g. nf-amazon,nf-tower
Specify the container image for the Nextflow pod
Set process options. Syntax: -process.key=value (Default: {}
Choose a configuration profile
Max number of processes that can be executed in parallel by each executor
Choose a configuration profile in the remoteConfig
Execute the script using the cached results, useful to continue executions that was stopped by an error
Revision of the project to run (either a git branch, tag or commit SHA number)
Execute the workflow replacing process scripts with command stubs (Default: false)
Test a script function with the name specified
Private repository user name
Volume claim mounts eg. my-pvc:/mnt/path
Enable process execution in a Charliecloud container runtime
Use the specified Conda environment package or file (must end with .yml|.yaml suffix)
Create pipeline DAG file
Enable process execution in a Docker container
Send a notification email on workflow completion to the specified recipients
Enable process execution in a Podman container
Create processes execution html report
Enable process execution in a Singularity container
Create processes execution timeline file
Monitor workflow execution with Seqera Tower service
Create processes execution tracing file
Send workflow status messages via HTTP to target URL

Disable process execution in a Podman container
Directory where intermediate result files are stored
Print the command usage (Default: false)

subcommands.name: log
subcommands.description: Print executions log and runtime info

options.description: Show log entries for runs executed after the specified one
Show log entries for runs executed before the specified one
Show log entries of all runs except the specified one
Comma separated list of fields to include in the printed log -- Use the `-l` option to show the list of available fields
Filter log entries by a custom expression e.g. process =~ /foo.*/ && status == 'COMPLETED'
Print the command usage (Default: false)
Show all available fields (Default: false)
Show only run names (Default: false)
Character used to separate column values (Default: )
Text template used to each record in the log
Update all downloaded projects (Default: false)
Print the command usage (Default: false)
Service hub where the project is hosted
Revision of the project to run (either a git branch, tag or commit SHA number)
Private repository user name

subcommands.name: secrets
subcommands.description: Handle and manage sensitive information for pipeline execution in a safe manner

options.description: List secrets available in the current store e.g. nextflow secrets list
Allows retrieving a secret value e.g. nextflow secrets get FOO
Allows creating creating a new secret or overriding an existing one e.g. nextflow secrets set FOO "Hello world"
Allows deleting an existing secret e.g. nextflow secrets delete FOO

subcommands.name: self-update
subcommands.description: Update nextflow runtime to the latest available version

options.description: Print the command usage (Default: false)
Print the command usage (Default: false)
List repository content (Default: false)
Hide header line (Default: false)
Use the specified configuration file(s) overriding any defaults
Set JVM properties
Execute nextflow in background
Add the specified file to configuration set
Launch nextflow via Docker (experimental)
Print this help
Set nextflow log file path
Do not print information messages
Send logs to syslog server (eg. localhost:514)
Print the program version
